title: How to Keep Shipping When You Walk Away from Your Desk — Zack Proser, WorkOS
summary: Zack Proser presents a four-layer stack for asynchronous agent-driven development: signal agents for Slack/Linear notifications, verification gates using lint and critic passes, weekly agent-driven conversation history review for skill generation, and Oura ring integration for well-being feedback. The core thesis is that human attention remains the bottleneck, not agent speed, and the solution involves voice-first coding with remote mobile oversight of agents dispatched to isolated git worktrees.
writeup: Zack Proser of WorkOS lays out a four-layer stack for running agent-driven development asynchronously, away from the keyboard: signal agents that filter Slack and Linear notifications, verification gates that run lint and critic passes, weekly review of conversation history to generate new skills, and Oura ring integration to fold well-being data into the loop. The framing argument is that agent speed is no longer the constraint; human attention is, so the workflow centers on voice-first coding and mobile oversight of agents dispatched to isolated git worktrees. Takeaways include using critic passes and logic gates as automated checkpoints, mining past conversations to compound capability over time, and treating remote control of running agents as the way to keep work moving while stepping away.
